data.title: CRWV, BE: CoreWeave, Bloom Energy Stocks Drop on Oracle–OpenAI Project Update
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV), an AI cloud computing provider, and Bloom Energy (BE), which supplies power systems for data centers, both closed lower on Friday after a Bloomberg report raised concerns about a major artificial intelligence data center project in Texas.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bloom Energy fell 15% to close at $135.19, while CoreWeave declined 2.5% to $72.99 by the end of Friday's session.

According to the report, Oracle (ORCL) and privately held OpenAI have scrapped plans to expand a large artificial intelligence data center in Abilene, Texas, after financing challenges emerged for the project. The site is part of the Stargate AI project, a roughly 1,000-acre data center campus being developed by Crusoe in Abilene. While parts of the facility are already running, Oracle and OpenAI reportedly chose not to move ahead with a large expansion.

Why Bloom Energy and CoreWeave Stocks Fell

The report weighed on companies linked to the AI data center buildout.

Bloom Energy dropped sharply as investors worried that canceling a large AI data center expansion could reduce demand for power systems used to run these facilities. The company provides fuel-cell power solutions often used by large data centers.

At the same time, CoreWeave also traded lower as the company is closely linked to the AI infrastructure boom. Any sign that hyperscalers or AI developers might slow or rethink large-scale data center projects can quickly impact sentiment around companies building AI compute capacity.

However, the report does not necessarily signal weaker AI demand. In fact, Meta Platforms (META) is said to be considering leasing the Abilene facility instead, with Nvidia (NVDA) helping facilitate talks with developer Crusoe. Even so, the uncertainty around the project was enough to pressure some AI infrastructure stocks on Friday.

Which Stock Is Best to Buy Now?

We use the TipRanks Stock Comparison tool to see how Wall Street analysts are rating these above-mentioned five stocks, and which company they believe has the strongest upside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Morning News Wrap-Up 3/6/26: Friday's Biggest Stock Market Stories!
data.description:
Friday is here, and with it comes one last round of stock market stories to close out the trading week. Dig into those with a breakdown of the top market happenings below.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

  • Cre8 Enterprise (CRE) stock rocketed higher after the financial services company regained compliance with listing requirements.
  • Airline stocks fell alongside reports of increased fuel prices due to the war in the Middle East.
  • Powell Max (PMAX) stock took a beating alongside a secondary offering of the shares.
  • The Dow Jones (DJIA) slipped after the latest jobs report came in worse than expected.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) stock was on the move after the tech giant failed to impress investors with its Project Helix Xbox reveal.
  • Microsoft also received praise from Jefferies as the winner of the AI race.
  • Mobix Labs (MOBX) stock was on the rise after the defense company announced a new purchase order.
  • Oracle (ORCL) jumped alongside reports of incoming layoffs at the technology company.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares could be affected by plans to change export rules on chips.
  • AMD stock was also up today despite a lackluster analyst update.
  • Sable Offshore (SOC) stock surged on reports that the U.S. government may overrule California's oil drilling restrictions.
  • The S&P 500 (SPX) was down as the unemployment rate in the U.S. increased to 4.4%.
  • United Airlines (UAL) stock slipped after the air travel company announced a new headphones policy.
  • BP (BP) stock was up after the oil giant revealed pay details for its new CEO.
  • The United States Oil Fund LP (USO) ETF gained today as investors flock to U.S. energy stocks.
  • President Donald Trump has urged Iran to agree to an unconditional surrender, with no plans to strike a deal on nuclear energy.
  • Peraso (PRSO) stock soared after the semiconductor company revealed an Israeli defense contract.
  • Versant Media Group (VSNT) stock was on the rise alongside the company's first earnings report.
  • Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOGL), and Nvidia (NVDA) data centers have become targets in the war in the Middle East.
data.author: William White

data.title: A Top Wall Street Analyst Remains Unimpressed with AMD Stock
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock was up on Friday even after the chipmaker's shares were hit with a lackluster analyst update. Five-star Bernstein analyst Stacy Rasgon assigned the company a Hold rating with a $235 price target, which represents a potential 18.5% upside for the shares.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Rasgon's neutral stance on AMD stock isn't too surprising. The analyst has long had a Hold rating for AMD shares, largely due to his belief that the artificial intelligence (AI) boom has overvalued the company. Even so, the analyst did recently comment that it was surprising AMD experienced such a sharp drop following its earnings report last month.

Rasgon is no stranger to the semiconductor space. He covers a wide variety of chipmakers, giving his opinion on AMD considerable weight. Here's a quick look at his coverage of other semiconductor stocks:

  • Intel (INTC) — Hold rating with a $36 price target, suggesting a 21.02% downside.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) — Buy rating with a $300 price target, implying a 63.9% upside.
  • Broadcom (AVGO) — Buy rating with a $425 price target, representing a 49.59% upside.
  • Qualcomm (QCOM) — Buy rating with a $175 price target, suggesting a 25.44% upside.
  • Applied Materials (AMAT) — Buy rating with a $425 price target, implying an 18.79% upside.

AMD Stock Movement Today

AMD stock experienced a slight rally on Friday but was still down 6.81% year-to-date. Even so, the company's shares have increased 98.83% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Cathie Wood Ends a Rocky Week with These Trades – HOOD, JOBY, ROKU, DKNG, JD
data.description:
Wall Street wrapped up a turbulent week as escalating U.S.–Iran tensions and the latest U.S. jobs report kept investors on edge. As volatility gripped Wall Street, Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made several portfolio changes on Friday, March 6, according to the firm's daily trade disclosures. The well-known investor executed multiple buy and sell trades across technology, fintech, and aviation stocks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's a quick look at ARK's latest moves.

What Did Cathie Wood Buy on March 6?

Joby Aviation (JOBY) – Wood made one of the day's largest purchases in the electric air-taxi company, buying 289,417 shares worth about $2.78 million. The move shows ARK's continued focus on the emerging electric aviation sector. The company recently reported its earnings results, posting a Q4 2025 loss of $0.14 per share, narrower than the $0.23 loss a year earlier. Following the results, Cathie Wood has been accumulating Joby shares.

Robinhood Markets (HOOD) – ARK also increased its stake in the fintech platform, purchasing 19,206 shares valued at roughly $1.55 million. The firm capitalized on the stock's roughly 4% drop on Friday.

JD Logistics (HK:2618) – The firm bought 1,129,547 shares of the logistics company for about $1.48 million, highlighting interest in tech-driven logistics businesses. The move came as the stock jumped roughly 22% on Friday, making it one of the day's notable gainers.

DraftKings (DKNG) – ARK added 10,600 shares of the sports-betting company, valued at around $269,876.

Here's What Wood Sold

Roku (ROKU) – The largest trade of the day was the sale of 32,304 shares worth about $3.17 million across several ARK funds. The move continues ARK's recent trend of trimming its Roku position.

Iridium Communications (IRDM) – ARK sold 86,890 shares of the satellite communications company for roughly $2.08 million.

10x Genomics (TXG) – Wood also reduced exposure to the genomics firm, selling 75,007 shares valued at about $1.62 million.

How Wall Street Views These Stocks

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

Currently, Robinhood, DraftKings, and Roku carry a Strong Buy rating from analysts, while JD Logistics and 10x Genomics are rated Moderate Buy. Among them, Robinhood offers the highest upside potential at about 65% over the next 12 months.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Traders Boost United States Oil Fund LP Stock (USO) on a Gulf Energy Production Warning
data.description:
The United States Oil Fund LP (USO) exchange-traded fund (ETF) rallied on Friday as stock traders turned to the crude oil-focused ETF. The reason for this is the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, which hit a turning point last weekend when the U.S. joined Israel in a missile strike against Iran. Iran has retaliated since then, and it's caused trouble for oil companies in the region.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

QatarEnergy, a prominent supplier of liquefied natural gas, said that it can't reach agreements with its customers due to the conflict. The company had to shut down production at some of its facilities earlier this week following a strike from Iran. Now, it has declared force majeure, which is a contract clause that excuses it from meeting obligations for unforeseen events, such as war.

Qatar Energy Minister Saad al-Kaabi, who's also the CEO of QatarEnergy, said the situation is only going to get worse moving forward. He warned that the country expects all exporters in the Gulf to declare force majeure within the next few days if the conflict with Iran doesn't come to an end. That would be a major disruption to the world's energy supply, resulting in higher prices for consumers.

What This Means for United States Oil Fund LP

With energy production in the Middle East disrupted, traders are looking for U.S. energy companies to invest in. This already led several energy stocks to rally earlier this week. Now, it appears that traders have taken an interest in ETFs as an easy way to bet on the sector in light of the war overseas.

United States Oil Fund LP has benefited from this today, as the ETF has gained 9.49% this morning. This builds on a 32.29% rally year-to-date and a 27.01% gain over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam on Labor Market Woes as Trump Calls for Iran's 'Unconditional Surrender'
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed in negative territory on Friday after the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published February's jobs report as the U.S.-Iran war entered its seventh day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Nonfarm payrolls fell by 92,000 in February, well below the consensus estimate of 55,000 additions.
  • The BLS also revised January and December's nonfarm payrolls lower by a combined 69,000 jobs.
  • The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% from 4.3% in January.
  • President Trump urged Iran to surrender and turned down the idea of signing a deal with the country. "There will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER!" he said in a Truth Social post.
  • BlackRock (BLK) was one of the worst performers in the S&P 500 (SPX) after the firm limited withdrawals in one of its flagship private credit funds.
  • Oracle (ORCL) closed lower following a report that it had scrapped plans to expand an AI data center in collaboration with OpenAI in Texas.
  • AMD (AMD) fell after TipRanks five-star analyst Stacy Rasgon warned that tighter U.S. export restrictions could slow global AI adoption.
  • Nvidia (NVDA), alongside the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX), also posted losses on the day.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Consumer staples were the best-performing sector on Friday as investors took on a risk-off approach amid declines in the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX). Consumer staples stocks are seen as defensive because demand for essential goods like food, beverages, and household products tends to remain stable even during economic slowdowns. In addition, companies in the industry are often able to pass down costs to consumers because their products are viewed as indispensable instead of discretionary.

Several stocks led the consumer staples sector's gains, including:

  • Costco (COST)
  • Bunge (BG)
  • Kroger (KR)
  • Clorox (CLX)
  • Kimberly-Clark (KMB)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

That brings us to the consumer discretionary sector, which was the worst-performing group. With a rising unemployment rate and higher gas prices, spending on travel, dining out, and entertainment could take a toll as consumers choose to stay in and conserve money. In addition, the ongoing U.S.-Iran war could dampen consumer sentiment and weigh on retailers and other discretionary businesses.

Notable consumer discretionary stocks trading lower include:

  • Carnival (CCL)
  • Chipotle (CMG)
  • Ralph Lauren (RL)
  • MGM Resorts (MGM)
  • Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: United Airlines (UAL) Stock Slides as the Airline Takes a Firm Stance on Headphones
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) stock fell on Friday after the air travel company said it was done with the noise. The company has updated its official policy to expand the number of things that can get passengers kicked off its flights. The latest entry on this list is failing to use headphones while listening to audio or video content.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Passenger complaints about loud noise during flights have increased, and United Airlines agrees that it has gone too far. What was once proper etiquette has been ignored by some passengers who have taken to playing music through Bluetooth speakers, making flights uncomfortable for others. To avoid further issues, United Airlines has taken this firm stance on headphone use. Carnival (CCL) took a similar step to cut down on passenger complaints when the cruise line banned Bluetooth speakers from its ships last year.

As the policy is new, there will likely be some issues as passengers who prefer not to use headphones adjust to the change. However, those who don't have headphones, or who forgot them, won't have to worry. United Airlines has announced that it will offer a free pair of headphones to passengers who don't have their own in an effort to alleviate future conflicts or complaints.

United Airlines Stock Movement

United Airlines stock was down 4.62% on Friday, extending a 14.66% year-to-date drop. Even so, the shares were still up 15.14% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Market Thinks AI Will Kill Brokers while Schwab Is Using it to Win
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI) is triggering a familiar fear across Wall Street that technology will eventually make traditional financial intermediaries obsolete. In the brokerage industry, that narrative has become particularly popular. If AI can analyze markets, generate trading strategies, and automate portfolio construction, then surely the role of the broker must shrink over time.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

But that assumption misunderstands where value is actually created in modern brokerage platforms. The market's disruption narrative assumes that AI will replace brokers. The more realistic outcome may be that it reinforces the biggest ones. Instead of replacing firms like The Charles Schwab Corporation (SCHW), AI is increasingly becoming a tool that strengthens their ecosystem.

Rather than losing relevance, Schwab — which saw its shares drop around 7% over the past month, versus a decline of just under 1% for the S&P 500 (SPX) — may be positioned to benefit precisely because it already sits at the center of the retail investing infrastructure. This view leaves me bullish on SCHW.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Fails to Impress Investors with Its Project Helix Xbox Reveal
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) stock was down on Friday following the reveal of Project Helix, the company's next-generation Xbox video game console. Asha Sharma, the new head of Xbox, revealed the codename of the console and provided some insight into it.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's a quick rundown of what we know about Project Helix.

  • Sharma confirmed that the next Xbox "will lead in performance." This echoes rumors that the device will be a premium product more powerful than the next PlayStation, but also likely more expensive.
  • It was also confirmed that Project Helix will be able to play Xbox and PC games, which is a feature that rumors have long claimed was coming to the next Xbox.
  • While not confirmed by Sharma, comments from former Xbox executives claimed that Project Helix will be backward compatible. This makes sense, as the video game company has focused on backward compatibility as a core feature of the Xbox brand.

Unfortunately, there's still a lot that we don't know about Project Helix. The exact specs of the game console are unknown, as are the price and release date. However, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) CEO Lisa Su did say she expects the console to be released in 2027. The timeframe she provided matters, as AMD is providing the chips for Project Helix.

Microsoft Stock Movement Today

Microsoft stock was down 1.14% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 1.35% rally yesterday. The shares have decreased 15.08% year-to-date but were up 4.42% over the past 12 months.

It's easy to underwent why new Xbox announcement wouldn't excite investors. The Xbox brand has struggled lately, leaving many without confidence in it. On top of that, the company recently underwent a controversial leadership change. All of this points to a lack of enthusiasm for the brand.
data.author: William White

data.title: Why Is Powell Max Stock (PMAX) Down Today?
data.description:
Powell Max (PMAX) stock plummeted on Friday after the financial communications and financial printing services company effected a secondary offering of its shares. This included 74,809,231 Class A ordinary shares held by selling shareholders. Powell Max has agreed to sell these shares on behalf of the selling shareholders.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Powell Max noted that it won't see any proceeds from the sale of these shares. The company also warned that the secondary offering could have a significant negative impact on PMAX stock, which explains why the shares have fallen today.

Powell Max noted that the shares included in this secondary offering come from the company's private placement in January 2025. Selling shareholders have the right to sell all or some of their shares and can do so across public or private transactions at prevailing market prices, at prices related to prevailing market prices, or at privately negotiated prices.

Powell Max Stock Sinks Today

Powell Max stock was down 31.18% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 3.63% fall yesterday. The shares have rallied 2.76% year-to-date but were down 39.14% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of PMAX stock, as some 106,000 shares changed hands. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 38,980 shares.
data.author: William White
